ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated black glutinous rice code. A00LC (glutinous rice, cooked) is the closest defensible match.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of black glutinous rice is approximately 200g (one bowl), as commonly consumed in Singapore as a side dish or dessert base.

nutrient_source.title: Black Glutinous Rice — Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB) Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database for Black Glutinous Rice (cr_id G00010095). All per_100g values are taken from the HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated at 0.9g/100g based on HPB total fat of 4.21g and typical fatty acid profile of black glutinous rice; monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat set to -1 as detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported in HPB source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — black rice / glutinous rice analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Singapore-style cooked black glutinous rice was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 59 is an analogous estimate based on published GI values for black rice (GI ~42–60) and glutinous rice variants reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). The mid-range value of 59 is used as a conservative estimate reflecting the intact bran layer of black glutinous rice which moderates glycaemic response relative to white glutinous rice (GI ~98). Glycemic load calculated as: GI × carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 59 × 50.38g / 100 = 29.7 (based on 200g serving with 25.19g carbohydrate per 100g).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 231mg/100g (borderline) offset by potassium 327mg/100g and dietary fibre 3.7g/100g
hypertension.guidance: Black glutinous rice has borderline sodium at 231mg/100g but a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio (0.71:1) consistent with DASH diet principles; patients with hypertension should consume plain preparations without added coconut milk, palm sugar, or salt, and limit portion size to one bowl (200g) to keep sodium intake within 462mg per serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate 25.19g/100g with dietary fibre 3.7g/100g and estimated GI 59 (medium)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Black glutinous rice has an estimated medium GI of 59 and a glycaemic load of approximately 29.7 per 200g serving, which is high; patients with type 2 diabetes should limit portion size to 100–150g, choose plain preparations without added sugar or coconut milk, and pair with protein or non-starchy vegetables to further moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~0.9g/100g), zero trans fat, high dietary fibre 3.7g/100g, and anthocyanin-rich bran
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Black glutinous rice is a cardioprotective whole grain with low saturated fat (~0.9g/100g), zero trans fat, 3.7g/100g dietary fibre, and anthocyanins that have demonstrated LDL-lowering and anti-oxidative properties in peer-reviewed studies; it is suitable for cardiovascular disease patients when consumed plain without coconut milk or palm sugar additions.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anthocyanin-rich bran with documented anti-inflammatory properties and low saturated fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Black glutinous rice provides anthocyanins with documented anti-inflammatory properties and 3.7g/100g dietary fibre supporting gut microbiome immune regulation; avoid coconut milk-based preparations which add pro-inflammatory saturated fat.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Black Glutinous Rice (黑糯米, Pulut Hitam) is a whole-grain rice variety made from black and purple glutinous rice kernels that retain their anthocyanin-rich bran layer, giving the grain its distinctive deep purple-black colour. In Singapore and across Southeast Asia, it is slow-cooked or boiled until the grains become soft, sticky, and fragrant, producing a naturally sweet, chewy texture that is satisfying without being greasy. The dish is commonly consumed as a savoury side dish at lunch or dinner, or as the base for the popular dessert bubur pulut hitam (black glutinous rice porridge with coconut milk and palm sugar). Nutritionally, black glutinous rice provides 164 kcal per 100g, with 6.5g protein, 4.21g fat, 25.19g carbohydrate, 3.7g dietary fibre, and 231mg sodium. Its anthocyanin content gives it antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties superior to white rice. The dietary fibre moderates glycaemic response, and the favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio (231mg sodium vs 327mg potassium per 100g) supports cardiovascular and blood pressure health. Clinically, it is suitable for most adults when consumed plain and in controlled portions, but sweetened coconut milk preparations significantly alter its nutritional profile and are not recommended for patients with diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010095, confidence 0.95) which is the primary approved source for Singapore dishes. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature, documented as such. Multilingual names are well-established for this food.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for black glutinous rice is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database as of 2026-04-16. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in HPB source. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient detail beyond the HPB-reported fields not available in the HPB Foodo source for this record. trans_fat_g set to 0.0: black glutinous rice is a minimally processed whole grain with no added fats; trans fat is confirmed absent. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as confirmed by HPB source — this is a plant-based grain food naturally containing no cholesterol. saturated_fat_g of 0.9g/100g is estimated from total fat 4.21g using the typical fatty acid profile of black glutinous rice (approximately 21% saturated); this is an adaptation noted in nutrient_source. GI of 59 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Singapore-style cooked black glutinous rice was found in the University of Sydney GI Database.
